/*
 * FUSION: ZERO-COPY LANGUAGE SERVER (SELF-OPTIMIZING)
 * ---------------------------------------------------
 * A persistent, adaptive LSP implementation.
 * * UPGRADES:
 * 1. Persistent Loop: Handles continuous stream of requests.
 * 2. Self-Optimizing L1 Cache: Learns frequently accessed symbols to reduce 
 * contention on the main Shared Memory (which is heavily used by the Builder).
 * 3. Predictive Caching: Promotes high-frequency items to local memory.
 * 4. State Revisioning: Invalidates local cache when Build/Audit updates occur.
 */

use std::sync::{Arc, RwLock};
use std::collections::HashMap;
use std::time::{Instant, Duration};
use tokio::time::sleep;
// Assuming DashMap is available in the crate root, otherwise standard RwLock<HashMap> is used
// for a self-contained example, we use standard library constructs wrapped for concurrency.
use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicUsize, Ordering};

// Reuse the state definition from main
// In production, this would be a shared crate `fusion_core`
use crate::fusion_unified_main::{FusionState, PackageId, BuildStatus}; 

/// Tracks usage statistics to inform optimization decisions
struct LspOptimizer {
    query_frequency: HashMap<String, u64>,
    total_queries: u64,
}

impl LspOptimizer {
    fn new() -> Self {
        Self {
            query_frequency: HashMap::new(),
            total_queries: 0,
        }
    }

    fn record_access(&mut self, key: &str) {
        self.total_queries += 1;
        let count = self.query_frequency.entry(key.to_string()).or_insert(0);
        *count += 1;
    }

    fn should_cache(&self, key: &str) -> bool {
        // Optimization Heuristic: 
        // Only cache items accessed more than 3 times to save memory/copying,
        // unless they are extremely hot relative to total queries.
        if let Some(count) = self.query_frequency.get(key) {
            return *count > 2;
        }
        false
    }
}

pub struct LspServer {
    state: Arc<RwLock<FusionState>>,
    // Local optimization state (internal mutability via RwLock)
    optimizer: RwLock<LspOptimizer>,
    // L1 Cache: Local copy of data to avoid locking the global FusionState 
    // during high-contention build phases. Map<PackageName, RenderedResponse>
    l1_cache: RwLock<HashMap<String, String>>,
    // Tracks the last known state of the build to handle invalidation
    last_known_status: RwLock<String>,
}

impl LspServer {
    pub fn new(state: Arc<RwLock<FusionState>>) -> Self {
        Self { 
            state,
            optimizer: RwLock::new(LspOptimizer::new()),
            l1_cache: RwLock::new(HashMap::new()),
            last_known_status: RwLock::new("Init".to_string()),
        }
    }

    /// Starts the persistent JSON-RPC event loop
    pub async fn run(&self) {
        println!("[LSP] Server Online. Adaptive Optimization: ENABLED.");

        // SIMULATION: In a real app, this consumes `tokio::io::stdin()`
        // Here we simulate a stream of incoming user actions (hovers, go-to-def)
        // to demonstrate performance stability over time.
        let simulated_requests = vec![
            ("hover", "serde", 10),
            ("hover", "serde", 12), // Repeated access (Learning)
            ("hover", "log", 5),
            ("hover", "serde", 15), // Hot item (Should optimize)
            ("completion", "tokio", 1),
            ("hover", "openssl", 20),
            ("hover", "serde", 22), // Cached access
        ];

        let mut request_id = 0;
        
        // The Loop: Ensures the server handles multiple requests indefinitely
        loop {
            // Check for Invalidation (Has the build changed?)
            self.check_invalidation();

            // Pick a request from simulation or idle
            let (action, target, line) = if request_id < simulated_requests.len() {
                simulated_requests[request_id]
            } else {
                // Keep the server alive, waiting for IDE input
                sleep(Duration::from_secs(2)).await;
                continue;
            };

            // Process Request
            self.handle_request(action, target, line).await;
            
            request_id += 1;
            
            // Simulate human latency between keystrokes
            sleep(Duration::from_millis(200)).await;
        }
    }

    /// Checks if the global build state has changed, requiring a cache flush.
    fn check_invalidation(&self) {
        let current_status_str = {
            let lock = self.state.read().unwrap();
            format!("{:?}", lock.status)
        };

        let mut last_status = self.last_known_status.write().unwrap();
        
        // Simple invalidation logic: If status string changes, flush cache.
        // In prod, use a revision ID (u64).
        if *last_status != current_status_str {
            // Only flush if transitioning OUT of a stable state or INTO a new build
            if current_status_str.contains("Compiling") || current_status_str.contains("Resolving") {
                 let mut cache = self.l1_cache.write().unwrap();
                 if !cache.is_empty() {
                     println!("[LSP] Build State Changed ({:?} -> {:?}). Flushing L1 Cache.", *last_status, current_status_str);
                     cache.clear();
                 }
            }
            *last_status = current_status_str;
        }
    }

    async fn handle_request(&self, action: &str, target_pkg: &str, _line: u32) {
        let start = Instant::now();
        
        // 1. Record Telemetry for Self-Optimization
        {
            let mut opt = self.optimizer.write().unwrap();
            opt.record_access(target_pkg);
        }

        // 2. Check L1 Cache (Fast Path)
        // If we have cached this package's metadata locally, return instantly
        // without touching the global RwLock.
        {
            let cache = self.l1_cache.read().unwrap();
            if let Some(cached_response) = cache.get(target_pkg) {
                println!("[LSP] Request: {} {} (L1 CACHE HIT) - {:?}us", action, target_pkg, start.elapsed().as_micros());
                return;
            }
        }

        // 3. Cache Miss - Access Shared Memory (Slow Path)
        // We acquire a Read lock. If the builder is writing, this waits.
        let response = {
            // Scope the lock tightly
            let lock = match self.state.read() {
                Ok(guard) => guard,
                Err(_) => {
                    eprintln!("[LSP] Error: Shared state poisoned.");
                    return;
                }
            };

            // Query the graph
            // Note: In real logic, we'd lookup by exact version or find best match
            let found = lock.artifacts.iter().find(|(k, _)| k.name == target_pkg);

            match found {
                Some((_, artifact)) => {
                    format!("Package: {}\nBinary: {:?}\nStatus: {:?}", 
                        artifact.package.name, artifact.binary_path, lock.status)
                }
                None => "Resolving...".to_string(),
            }
        };

        // 4. Adaptive Optimization Step
        // If this item is "Hot", promote it to L1 Cache
        let should_cache = {
            let opt = self.optimizer.read().unwrap();
            opt.should_cache(target_pkg)
        };

        if should_cache {
            let mut cache = self.l1_cache.write().unwrap();
            // Double check to avoid overwriting newer data if race occurred
            if !cache.contains_key(target_pkg) {
                println!("[LSP] OPTIMIZER: Promoting '{}' to L1 Cache (Hot Item).", target_pkg);
                cache.insert(target_pkg.to_string(), response.clone());
            }
        }

        println!("[LSP] Request: {} {} (SHARED MEMORY READ) - {:?}us", action, target_pkg, start.elapsed().as_micros());
    }
}

pub async fn run_lsp_server(state: Arc<RwLock<FusionState>>) {
    let server = LspServer::new(state);
    server.run().await;
}
